intitule: Vocabulary: Likes and Dislikes at School

enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Complete the sentences with the correct verb: like, love, hate, don't like

Choose the correct word to complete each sentence about school.
images: A happy student holding a book and smiling.

questions.texte: I _______ Maths because it is fun.
She _______ History because it is boring.
They _______ English because it is interesting.
My brother _______ Science because it is difficult.

enonce.texte: Exercise 2: Match the expressions with their meanings

questions.texte: Match the expression with its meaning.

sousQuestions.reponse: c. I really like it very much
d. I don't like it at all

intitule: Reading Comprehension: Expressing Likes and Dislikes

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: Read the text and answer the questions

Hello! My name is Amina. I am in Class Six. I love English because it is very interesting. I like Science because it is fun. I don't like History because it is boring. I hate Maths because it is very difficult. My favourite subject is Art because I like drawing. I like my school very much.
images: A young African girl in a school uniform reading a book.

questions.texte: What subject does Amina love?
Why does she like Science?
What subject does she not like?
Which subject does she hate? Why?
questions.reponse: She hates Maths because it is very difficult.

questions.texte: What is her favourite subject? Why?
questions.reponse: Her favourite subject is Art because she likes drawing.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Complete the sentences with because + a reason

Use the words in parentheses to complete the sentences.

questions.texte: I like PE _______ (it / fun).
She doesn't like Science _______ (it / difficult).
They love English _______ (they / interesting).
questions.reponse: because it is interesting or because they find it interesting

questions.texte: I hate homework _______ (I / have / no time).

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Write about your school preferences

Write 3-5 sentences about what you like and don't like at school. Include reasons for your choices using "because".

Use these expressions: I like..., I don't like..., I love..., I hate...
images: A student writing in a notebook.

questions.texte: Write a short paragraph about your likes and dislikes at school.
questions.reponse: Example answer: I love English because it is very interesting. I like Science because it is fun and I learn new things. I don't like History because it is boring. I hate Maths because it is very difficult. My favourite subject is PE because I like sports.

intitule: Listening and Speaking: Interview Your Classmate

enonce.texte:
Exercise 6: Pair Work - Interview your classmate

Ask your classmate questions about their school preferences. Write their answers in the space below.

Questions to ask:

  • What subject do you love?
  • Why do you love it?
  • What subject do you hate?
  • Why do you hate it?
  • What is your favourite subject?
  • What is your least favourite subject?

questions.texte: Write your classmate's answers in complete sentences.
questions.reponse: Example answer: My classmate's name is Jean. He loves English because it is interesting. He hates Maths because it is difficult. His favourite subject is PE because he likes sports. His least favourite subject is History because it is boring.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 7: Write about your likes and dislikes

Write a short paragraph explaining what you like and dislike at school. Use the expressions learned and give reasons for each preference.

questions.texte: Write a paragraph about your likes and dislikes at school.
questions.reponse: Example answer: I like school because I learn many new things. I love English because it is very interesting and I like speaking English. I like Science because it is fun. I don't like History because there are many dates to remember. I hate homework because I have no time to play with my friends.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 8: Put the words in the correct order

Rearrange the words to form correct sentences about likes and dislikes.

questions.texte: I / English / because / love / interesting / it / is / .
questions.reponse: I love English because it is interesting.

questions.texte: don't / I / History / boring / because / like / it / is / .
questions.reponse: I don't like History because it is boring.

questions.texte: favourite / my / subject / is / PE / .
hates / Maths / brother / my / difficult / very / because / it / is / .
questions.reponse: My brother hates Maths because it is very difficult.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 9: Create a dialogue

Work with a partner. Create a short dialogue where you talk about your school preferences. Use expressions of likes and dislikes.

Use these expressions:

  • I like / I love / I don't like / I hate
  • because
  • What subject do you like?
  • Why do you like it?

questions.texte: Write your dialogue here.
questions.reponse:
Example dialogue:

Student A: Hi Marie! What subject do you like at school?

Student B: I love English because it is very interesting. What about you?

Student A: I like Science because it is fun. I don't like History because it is boring.

Student B: I hate Maths because it is very difficult.

Student A: What is your favourite subject?

Student B: My favourite subject is Art because I like drawing.

Student A: That's great! My favourite subject is PE because I like sports.

intitule: Creative Writing: Letter to a Friend

enonce.texte:
Exercise 10: Write a letter to a friend

Write a short letter to a friend telling them about your favourite and least favourite subjects at school. Explain why you like or dislike each subject.

Use these expressions:

  • I like / I love / I don't like / I hate
  • because
  • My favourite subject is...
  • My least favourite subject is...

questions.texte: Write a letter to a friend.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Dear Kofi,

How are you? I want to tell you about my school. I love English because it is very interesting. I also like Science because it is fun. My favourite subject is Art because I like drawing pictures. I don't like History because it is boring. I hate Maths because it is very difficult and I don't understand it well. What subjects do you like? Please write back soon.

Your friend,
Amina
